message:
feat(sqlmodel): add discriminator attribute for union type support

Implements discriminator field support for Pydantic-compatible union type
discrimination, enabling proper JSON Schema generation and OpenAPI spec
compatibility for tagged unions.

New features:
- FieldInfo.discriminator: Optional field storing the discriminator name
- FieldInfo.discriminator() / discriminator_opt(): Builder methods
- Parse #[sqlmodel(discriminator = "field_name")] attribute on fields

Implementation details:
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/field.rs:
  - Added discriminator field to FieldInfo struct with comprehensive docs
  - Added discriminator() and discriminator_opt() builder methods
  - Added test_field_info_discriminator() test

- crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/parse.rs:
  - Extended FieldDef with discriminator field
  - Extended FieldAttrs to parse discriminator attribute
  - Added parse_field_attrs handling for discriminator = "value"
  - Updated allowed attributes list in error message
  - Added comprehensive tests:
    - test_discriminator_field_parsed
    - test_discriminator_requires_string_literal
    - test_discriminator_with_alias

- crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/lib.rs:
  - Updated code generation to emit discriminator_opt() call

Usage example:
```rust
#[derive(Model)]
struct Owner {
    #[sqlmodel(discriminator = "pet_type")]
    pet: PetUnion, // PetUnion has #[serde(tag = "pet_type")]
}
```

This enables JSON Schema generators to emit proper discriminator mappings
for OpenAPI specs when models contain tagged union fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
